root_text.sn47.1:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati ambapālivane.
root_text.sn47.1:2.1: “Ekāyanvāyaṁ, bhikkhave, maggo sattānaṁ visuddhiyā sokaparidevānaṁ samatikkamāya dukkhadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamāya ñāyassa adhigamāya nibbānassa sacchikiriyāya, yadidaṁ—cattāro satipaṭṭhānā.
root_text.sn47.1:2.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.1:2.4: vedanāsu vedanānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.1:2.5: citte cittānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.1:2.6: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.1:2.7: Ekāyanvāyaṁ, bhikkhave, maggo sattānaṁ visuddhiyā sokaparidevānaṁ samatikkamāya dukkhadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamāya ñāyassa adhigamāya nibbānassa sacchikiriyāya, yadidaṁ—cattāro satipaṭṭhānā”ti.
root_text.sn47.1:3.2: Attamanā te bhikkhū bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinandunti.

translation_text.sn47.1:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, in Ambapālī’s Mango Grove.
translation_text.sn47.1:1.3: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.sn47.1:2.1: “Mendicants, the four kinds of mindfulness meditation are the path to convergence. They are in order to purify sentient beings, to get past sorrow and crying, to make an end of pain and sadness, to discover the system, and to realize extinguishment.
translation_text.sn47.1:2.3: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.1:2.4: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.1:2.5: They meditate observing an aspect of the mind—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.1:2.6: They meditate observing an aspect of principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.1:2.7: The four kinds of mindfulness meditation are the path to convergence. They are in order to purify sentient beings, to get past sorrow and crying, to make an end of pain and sadness, to discover the system, and to realize extinguishment.”
translation_text.sn47.1:3.1: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn47.1:3.2: Satisfied, the mendicants approved what the Buddha said.
